label: Table 2
title: Prevalence of behavioural addiction among OCD patients (n=150) and healthy controls (n=131).
source: Rai et al., 2022, Psychiatry Research Communications

columns.role: Each row is a different questionnaire domain; caseness uses that scale’s published cut-off
How many of the 150 OCD participants crossed the cut-off; % is within the OCD column only
Same for the 131 healthy controls—percent is within the control column only
Chi-square test statistic—bigger numbers (with enough people) mean a larger mismatch between columns than chance would usually produce
If p is small (e.g. <0.05), the yes/no rates differ between groups more than we would expect if there were no real gap

cellGuide: Read each percentage down its own column—that tells you how common a positive screen was inside that group. Chi-square then asks whether the two columns’ split looks meaningfully different overall. A large χ² with a tiny p (like internet here) is the statistical way of saying “these bars are not close.”
